name: Sklearn K-Means
description: K-Means clustering algorithm using scikit-learn. K-Means is an unsupervised learning algorithm that partitions observations into K distinct clusters based on feature similarity, without using labeled data.
input: It takes in a DataFrame as input
output: Outputs cluster assignments, cluster centers, and evaluation metrics such as inertia and silhouette score

fields.name: n_clusters
fields.title: Number of Clusters
fields.description: Specifies the number of clusters (K) to form. Each cluster represents a group of similar data points. If 'Estimate K' is enabled, this value is ignored and automatically determined.

fields.name: estimate_k
fields.title: Estimate K
fields.description: Enables automatic estimation of the optimal number of clusters by evaluating multiple K values over a specified search range.

fields.name: k_search_range
fields.title: K Search Range
fields.description: Defines the inclusive range of K values (for example, '2,15') to be evaluated when estimating the optimal number of clusters.

fields.name: optimization_metric
fields.title: Optimization Metric
fields.description: Metric used to select the best K. Silhouette Score maximizes cluster separation, while Inertia minimizes within-cluster variance.

fields.name: featureCols
fields.title: Feature Columns
fields.description: Specifies the input feature columns used for clustering. All selected columns must be numeric or vector-compatible. If empty, all eligible columns are used.

fields.name: init
fields.title: Initialization Mode
fields.description: Determines how initial cluster centroids are selected. 'k-means++' spreads out initial centers to speed up convergence, while 'random' selects centers randomly.

fields.name: n_init
fields.title: Number of Initializations
fields.description: Number of times the K-Means algorithm is run with different centroid seeds. The final model is chosen based on the best inertia score.

fields.name: max_iter
fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum number of iterations allowed for a single run of the K-Means algorithm before stopping.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerance
fields.description: Relative tolerance for declaring convergence based on changes in cluster center positions.

fields.name: random_state
fields.title: Random State
fields.description: Seed value used to control random number generation for reproducible clustering results.

fields.name: algorithm
fields.title: Algorithm
fields.description: Algorithm used to compute K-Means. 'lloyd' is the classical approach, while 'elkan' is faster for datasets with well-separated clusters.

fields.name: saveCentroidsPath
fields.title: Save Centroids Path
fields.description: Filesystem path where the computed cluster centroids are saved in CSV format for downstream analysis or reuse.
